title: Therapy-related AML with TP53 mutation after follicular lymphoma in a 76-year-old woman with recent traumatic ICH

patient.chief_complaint: Incidentally detected leukocytosis with blasts and pancytopenia during stroke-unit admission for traumatic intracerebral hemorrhage.
patient.vital_signs: Temp 37.8 °C, BP 98/60 mmHg, HR 112/min (irregular), RR 21/min, SpO₂ 93% on room air.

1.setting: Acute stroke-unit admission with incidentally discovered cytopenia and evolving leukocytosis with circulating blasts.
1.capabilities: You can review clinical presentation, recent trauma and ICH, initial blood counts, chemistry, and baseline imaging. You should recognize hyperleukocytic acute leukemia with laboratory tumor lysis and coagulopathy in a frail patient and decide on immediate stabilization, diagnostic workup, and whether intensive induction is realistic.

2.setting: Hematologic workup after bone marrow biopsy and cytogenetics.
2.capabilities: You now have full marrow morphology, flow cytometry defining an AML with monocytic component, and complex karyotype. You should classify the leukemia, integrate recent intracerebral hemorrhage and comorbidities, and select an induction concept (intensive vs lower-intensity) while considering bleeding risk.

3.setting: Post-NGS risk stratification and definitive treatment planning.
3.capabilities: You know the NGS profile including TP53, EZH2, and CBL mutations with low-level FLT3-TKD, confirming ELN 2024 adverse risk AML-pCT. You should finalize the non-transplant treatment strategy (including venetoclax-based low-intensity regimens), define consolidation, and outline supportive care, explicitly arguing against alloSCT.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, and social context.
demographics.content:
Age: 76 years
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 2
Comorbidities: B-NHL history, hypothyroidism, gastroesophageal reflux disease, diverticulosis of the sigmoid colon, small hiatal hernia, history of multiple abdominal surgeries with short bowel syndrome, multiple hepatic cysts, osteoporosis, polyneuropathy, non-smoker, moderate alcohol consumption.
Social history: Husband deceased, no children, retired engineer. Remains physically active within limits of polyneuropathy and short-bowel symptoms.
Height: 174 cm; Bodyweight: 58 kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for context of presentation, neurological course, and physical exam.
history_physical.content:
On 2024-09-21, the patient was admitted to the stroke unit after a fall from a beer bench at the Oktoberfest, resulting in a head injury and transient speech arrest. Cranial CT revealed a small left frontal intracerebral hemorrhage (< 1.5 cm) without mass effect or midline shift, most likely traumatic.
Neurologically, she presented with transient dysphasia, mild right-sided facial weakness, and a single generalized tonic-clonic seizure during admission. EEG showed no epileptiform discharges. Over subsequent days, neurological deficits regressed almost completely. She regained fluent speech and independent ambulation, with only mild residual word-finding difficulty. Cognitive testing demonstrated slowed processing speed and impaired short-term memory in the context of disrupted circadian rhythm and fragmented sleep.
During hospitalization, routine laboratory testing incidentally revealed marked cytopenia (Hb 8.9 g/dL, Plt 82 × 10⁹/L, WBC 2.1 × 10⁹/L), prompting hematologic evaluation. Repeat testing showed rapidly rising leukocytosis (WBC 14.1 × 10⁹/L) with 25% circulating blasts. Clinically, the patient reported only fatigue, intermittent diarrhea secondary to short-bowel syndrome, and chronic reflux symptoms.
Current medication: Levothyroxine 100 µg 1-0-0, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, magnesium supplementation 1-0-0, loperamide on demand, levetiracetam 500 mg 1-0-1, pregabalin 125 mg 1-0-1, Kreon 25 000 U 1-1-1.
Vital signs: BP 98/60 mmHg, HR 112/min (irregular), Temp 37.8 °C, RR 21/min, SpO₂ 93% (room air).

cbc_chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for initial blood counts, differential, tumor lysis markers, and organ function.
cbc_chemistry.content:
CBC: Hb 8.6 g/dL, WBC 44 × 10⁹/L, Plt 12 × 10⁹/L; MCV 89 fL, MCH 29 pg; differential: 30% blasts, 60% monocytes, 8% neutrophils, 2% lymphocytes.
Coagulation: aPTT 42 sec, INR 2.1, fibrinogen 333 mg/dL, AT3 77%, FXIII 54%.
Na 124 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL, BUN 25 mg/dL, AST 132 U/L, ALT 163 U/L, bilirubin 2.4 mg/dL, LDH 1289 U/L, uric acid 9.9 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.85 mmol/L, phosphate 4.2 mg/dL, lactate 5.3 mmol/L, CK 320 U/L, Troponin T 0.002 ng/mL, NT-proBNP 461 pg/mL.

baseline_imaging.valid_request_if: Requests for CT, ultrasound, echocardiography, and chest imaging.
baseline_imaging.content:
Abdominal ultrasound (2024-09-27): Mild hepatomegaly (liver span 16.8 cm) with increased echogenicity consistent with steatosis hepatis. Several thin-walled hepatic cysts up to 3.2 cm, no septations. Gallbladder surgically absent. Intra- and extrahepatic bile ducts not dilated (DHC 5 mm). Spleen borderline enlarged (13.9 cm), homogeneous parenchyma. Kidneys horseshoe-shaped, normal cortical thickness and echogenicity, no hydronephrosis. No intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy. Small amount of free pelvic fluid.
Cranial CT (2024-09-27): Small left frontal intraparenchymal hemorrhage (1.1 cm) without mass effect or midline shift, unchanged from prior imaging. No acute ischemia. Chronic microangiopathic changes with scattered white-matter hypodensities.
Echocardiography (2024-09-27): LVEF 52%, no segmental wall motion abnormalities. Mild concentric LV hypertrophy. Mild mitral and tricuspid regurgitation (grade I°). Normal RV function (TAPSE 21 mm). No pericardial effusion.
Chest X-ray: Clear lung fields, no focal infiltrates or pleural effusion. Cardiac silhouette within normal limits. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for marrow morphology, flow cytometry, and histology.
bone_marrow.content:
The bone marrow was hypercellular (80%), showing extensive blast infiltration (35% of nucleated cells). The blasts were medium to large in size, with irregular nuclear contours, open chromatin, and prominent nucleoli. Auer rods were occasionally seen. Cytoplasm was moderately abundant, with fine azurophilic granules and sporadic pseudopodia. A second population of promonocytes and immature monocytoid cells was abundant, characterized by irregularly folded nuclei and basophilic cytoplasm. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was markedly reduced; megakaryocytes were rare and predominantly dysplastic (micromegakaryocytes, hypolobated forms). Erythropoiesis was markedly suppressed with megaloblastoid changes, and granulocytic dysplasia was evident (hypogranulated and pseudo–Pelger-Huët forms). No significant lymphoid or plasma-cell increase was observed.
Flow cytometry revealed a blast population (CD34⁺/CD33⁺) co-expressing CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, MPO, and showing aberrant CD19 positivity; negative for CD23, cyCD3, CD20.
A second population consisted of CD34^dim/CD33⁺ cells with CD117⁺, CD64⁺, CD11c⁺, partial CD14^dim, CD7⁺, and CD56⁺. Among lymphocytes, T cells accounted for 73% (CD3⁺), with CD4⁺ 43% and CD8⁺ 35% (CD4/CD8 = 1.2), without aberrant expression. B cells represented 12% (CD19⁺/CD20⁺, polyclonal with balanced κ/λ). NK cells comprised 15% (CD16⁺/CD56⁺).
Histology confirmed a hypercellular marrow (>80%) with diffuse and interstitial infiltration by blasts of immature monocytic morphology, largely replacing normal architecture. Scattered small lymphoid aggregates were present but not dominant. Reticulin staining demonstrated a mild-to-moderate increase in fibers (MF-2). Immunohistochemistry confirmed blast positivity for CD34, CD117, MPO, CD68 (subset), CD7 (partial), and CD56 (strong membranous).

cytogenetics_chimerism.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional karyotype and key structural abnormalities.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for infection workup and neuroimaging beyond baseline.
extended_diagnostics.content:
Infection screen: CRP 7.2 mg/L, PCT 0.4 ng/mL. Blood cultures remained negative. Serum galactomannan negative. Baseline chest CT showed no pulmonary infiltrates. Nasopharyngeal PCR positive for enterovirus and rhinovirus. Rectal/skin surveillance swabs positive for ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae with OXA-48 carbapenemase; VRE and MRSA not detected.
Cranial MRI (2024-09-28): Confirmed cortical microhemorrhage (1.1 cm) with mild perifocal edema, no new bleeding or ischemia, no contrast-enhancing or leptomeningeal lesions, no cerebral venous sinus thrombosis.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for NGS results and ELN-relevant mutations.
molecular_panel.content: TP53 mutation (VAF 13%), EZH2 mutation (VAF 21%), CBL mutation (VAF 14%), FLT3-TKD (ratio 0.008) detected. No pathogenic variants detected in FLT3-ITD, NPM1, CBFβ, RUNX1, CEBPA, IDH1, IDH2, ASXL1, DNMT3A, EZH2, SRSF2, SF3B1, U2AF1, ZRSR2, TET2, NRAS, KRAS, JAK2, or SETBP1. No rearrangements involving PML::RARA, CBFB::MYH11, RUNX1::RUNX1T1, BCR::ABL1, or KMT2A::MLLT3 were detected.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for clonally trackable markers and immunophenotypic populations.
mrd_status.content: Active disease, TP53, EZH2, FLT3-TKD and CBL mutations. Two populations identified by flow: (CD34⁺/CD33⁺) with co-expression of CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, MPO, CD19. A second population: CD34^dim/CD33⁺ with co-expression of CD117⁺, CD64⁺, CD11c⁺, partial CD14^dim, CD7⁺ & CD56⁺.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior lymphoma history and cytotoxic exposure.
treatment_history.content:
2012: Initial diagnosis of follicular lymphoma grade 1–2, Ann Arbor stage III, with predominant mesenteric manifestation. Managed conservatively with a watch-and-wait strategy due to low tumor burden.
07/23/2015: Rapid disease progression with acute kidney injury (creatinine 4.1 mg/dL) secondary to ureteral compression, requiring DJ stent insertion. Underwent emergency laparotomy with extended right hemicolectomy, resection of ~70 cm terminal ileum, and ureterolysis. Histology confirmed follicular lymphoma grade 2 (CD20⁺). The postoperative course was complicated by prolonged ileus, intra-abdominal abscess formation requiring drainage, and later enterocutaneous fistula formation with temporary stoma placement.
08/11/2015: Hematology referral for progressive lymphadenopathy and cytopenia. Treatment was initiated with rituximab (375 mg/m² day 1) plus bendamustine (90 mg/m² days 1–2) every 28 days for 6 cycles. The course was complicated by multiple episodes of febrile neutropenia, one episode of E. coli septicemia, and surgical site infection requiring re-laparotomy and stoma revision (01/12/2016). Post-therapy evaluation confirmed complete remission.
02/21/2020: Clinical relapse with recurrent abdominal lymphadenopathy and B-symptoms. Biopsy confirmed recurrent FL grade 2. Treated with 6 cycles of O-CHOP; vincristine discontinued after cycle 3 due to worsening neuropathy. Complications included mucositis grade II, transient cardiotoxicity (LVEF 50%), and pneumonia requiring IV antibiotics. Post-treatment PET-CT demonstrated complete metabolic remission. Maintenance with obinutuzumab (1000 mg q8 weeks) was administered for 1 year (until 02/2021), discontinued during the COVID-19 pandemic.
2022–2024: Sustained clinical remission. The patient discontinued regular hematologic follow-up, citing excellent well-being.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for concise disease label and risk category.
disease_status.content:
Relapse type: AML-pCT with TP53 Mutation. No FL relapse detected.
The patient has a long-standing history of follicular lymphoma with multiple prior treatment lines and now presents with secondary acute myeloid leukemia.

special_context.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual constraints such as age, ICH, comorbidities, and transplant feasibility.
special_context.content: The current presentation followed a trauma and was complicated by a minor intracerebral hemorrhage with transient stroke-like symptoms including speech disturbance and one brief seizure. Neurological recovery was nearly complete, though gait instability persists due to chronic polyneuropathy. Current diagnostic work-up demonstrates a proliferative AML with complex caryotype and TP53 mutation and low-allelic FLT3-TKD positivity. Performance status is ECOG 2. There is no clinical or radiologic evidence of active or recurrent follicular lymphoma. Due to advanced age and ECOG performance status, allogeneic stem cell transplantation is not recommended.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Acute leukemia with hyperleukocytosis, most likely AML pCT

treatment_plan.action: Initiate hydroxyurea for cytoreduction given hyperleukocytosis with WBC 44 x 10^9/L.
Start tumor lysis prophylaxis with rasburicase, aggressive hydration, and close electrolyte monitoring given elevated LDH and uric acid.
Transfuse platelets targeting approximately 50 x 10^9/L due to intracerebral hemorrhage and severe thrombocytopenia.
Obtain broad infection cultures (blood, urine, swabs, stool, serum galactomannan), chest CT, and extended infection lab diagnostics given fever, tachycardia, and elevated lactate.
Initiate broad-spectrum IV antibiotics given clinical suspicion for sepsis with hypotension, tachycardia, fever, and elevated lactate.
Obtain neurologic consultation for additional follow-up imaging and management of intracerebral hemorrhage.
Perform urgent bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with full immunophenotyping to confirm acute leukemia subtype and distinguish AML from transformed lymphoma.

key_findings_quotes: WBC 44 x 10^9/L with differential: 30% blasts, 60% monocytes, 8% neutrophils, 2% lymphocytes.
Plt 12 x 10^9/L with aPTT 42 sec, INR 2.1 indicating severe thrombocytopenia and coagulopathy.
LDH 1289 U/L, uric acid 9.9 mg/dL indicating highly proliferative disease with tumor lysis risk.
Small left frontal intraparenchymal hemorrhage (1.1 cm) without mass effect or midline shift.
BP 98/60 mmHg, HR 112/min (irregular), Temp 37.8 C, lactate 5.3 mmol/L suggesting sepsis.
No intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy, suggesting no B-NHL relapse.

treatment_plan.action: Order molecular diagnostics to determine targetable alterations and complete risk stratification based on complex karyotype.
Continue cytoreduction with hydroxyurea and tumor lysis prophylaxis given extensive blast infiltration.
Implement contact isolation according to local recommendations for ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae with OXA-48 carbapenemase.
Discontinue IV antibiotics if patient is clinically stable without signs of infection and switch to prophylaxis given negative cultures and imaging.
Continue monitoring of intracerebral hemorrhage with follow-up imaging and maintain platelet transfusion support.

key_findings_quotes: The bone marrow was hypercellular (80%), showing extensive blast infiltration (35% of nucleated cells). Auer rods were occasionally seen.
Flow cytometry revealed a blast population (CD34+/CD33+) co-expressing CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, MPO, and showing aberrant CD19 positivity.
CRP 7.2 mg/L, PCT 0.4 ng/mL. Blood cultures remained negative. Serum galactomannan negative.
Rectal/skin surveillance swabs positive for ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae with OXA-48 carbapenemase.
Confirmed cortical microhemorrhage (1.1 cm) with mild perifocal edema, no new bleeding or ischemia.

round_3.working_diagnosis: AML pCT with TP53 mutation, ELN 2024 adverse risk

treatment_plan.action: Initiate pre-phase with hydroxyurea 2x2g for cytoreduction, followed by induction with cladribine 5 mg/m2 IV days 1-5, low-dose cytarabine 20 mg SC twice daily days 1-10, and venetoclax 70 mg orally days 1-21.
Plan consolidation alternating between azacitidine 75 mg/m2 days 1-7 plus venetoclax 70 mg for 7-14 days, and cladribine 5 mg/m2 days 1-3 plus LDAC 20 mg SC twice daily days 1-10 plus venetoclax.
Do not pursue allogeneic stem cell transplantation due to advanced age, ECOG 2 status, and TP53-mutated adverse-risk disease.
Provide TLS prophylaxis with rasburicase and standard supportive measures including platelet transfusions to maintain counts around 50 x 10^9/L given intracerebral hemorrhage history.
Include social service assessment and palliative care team given gait instability, chronic polyneuropathy, and adverse-risk AML prognosis.

key_findings_quotes: Final diagnosis: AML-pCT with TP53 mutation. ELN 2024: adverse risk.
TP53 mutation (VAF 13%), EZH2 mutation (VAF 21%), CBL mutation (VAF 14%), FLT3-TKD (ratio 0.008) detected.
Reference treatment plan: Venetoclax added to cladribine plus low-dose cytarabine alternating with 5-azacitidine. Pre-phase with hydroxyurea 2x2g for a few days.
Due to advanced age and ECOG performance status, allogeneic stem cell transplantation is not recommended.
Supportive measures: TLS prophylaxis with rasburicase. Platelet transfusions to maintain platelet counts around 50 x 10^9/L in the setting of ICB.
2022-2024: Sustained clinical remission. No evidence for lymphoma relapse.
